evidence.speaker: patient
evidence.quote: Yes. I have a plan. I know what I would use and when. I can't promise I won't do it.

evidence.speaker: patient
evidence.quote: No. I don't think I can keep that promise. I don't trust myself right now.

evidence.speaker: nurse
evidence.quote: Patient will need one-to-one observation given active plan and inability to contract for safety.

evidence.speaker: nurse
evidence.quote: He remains on one-to-one observation. Overnight he was found attempting to tie a sheet around his neck and was redirected by staff.

evidence.speaker: patient
evidence.quote: I just want it to stop. I know I tried something last night. I can't guarantee I won't try again.

criteria.rationale: The patient presented with an active, specific suicide plan and explicitly refused to contract for safety. One-to-one observation was initiated on admission. On day three, the patient made a self-harm attempt on the unit (attempted ligature) and continues to express inability to guarantee safety.

evidence.speaker: patient
evidence.quote: My roommate moved out. There's no one there. It's been completely chaotic, I haven't slept in three days.

evidence.speaker: patient
evidence.quote: Not really. I had coffee today. I can't sit still long enough to eat.

evidence.speaker: nurse
evidence.quote: He still isn't sleeping. He was awake all night pacing and required two staff to manage behavioral agitation around 3 AM.

evidence.speaker: nurse
evidence.quote: He refused all meals yesterday and needed staff assistance to change clothes this morning.

criteria.rationale: The patient reported three days without sleep and minimal food intake on admission. Nursing reports continued sleep deprivation, refusal of all meals, need for staff assistance with basic self-care, and behavioral dyscontrol requiring two-staff management overnight — all indicating severe functional impairment.

evidence.speaker: patient
evidence.quote: My roommate moved out. There's no one there. It's been completely chaotic, I haven't slept in three days.

evidence.speaker: psychiatrist
evidence.quote: You were on a PHP nine days ago and decompensated within the week of leaving. That tells us a lower level of care is not going to hold right now.

evidence.speaker: social_worker
evidence.quote: He has no safe discharge environment. His roommate is gone, there is no family in the area, and he has no outpatient team currently established.

evidence.speaker: social_worker
evidence.quote: He explicitly left the PHP against staff advice on day three of that program, which also contributed to his rapid return.

criteria.rationale: The patient has no safe home environment (roommate gone, no family nearby, no outpatient team), decompensated within nine days of leaving a recent PHP — which he also left against staff advice. Active medication titration requires continued monitoring. Multiple specific factors indicate a lower level of care would likely fail.

evidence.speaker: psychiatrist
evidence.quote: We're going to start you on lithium and quetiapine. The lithium will need close monitoring while we titrate to a therapeutic level.

evidence.speaker: nurse
evidence.quote: Zero point four milliequivalents per liter. Still subtherapeutic. We are titrating upward per protocol.

evidence.speaker: psychiatrist
evidence.quote: So we are actively titrating the lithium and the quetiapine dose was increased last night. Neither medication is at therapeutic range yet.

evidence.speaker: psychiatrist
evidence.quote: The lithium level this morning was zero point six. Still not therapeutic. We're continuing the titration.

criteria.rationale: Lithium was newly started and remains subtherapeutic at day four (level 0.6 mEq/L). Quetiapine dose was increased. Both medications require continued inpatient titration with daily lab monitoring. A partial treatment response is emerging but acute symptoms persist.

evidence.speaker: psychiatrist
evidence.quote: You were on a PHP nine days ago and decompensated within the week of leaving. That tells us a lower level of care is not going to hold right now.

evidence.speaker: social_worker
evidence.quote: Given her living situation and the rapid return after PHP, step-down to outpatient or PHP again would be premature at this time.

evidence.speaker: psychiatrist
evidence.quote: The PHP failed nine days before admission. We cannot step him back down before the medications are therapeutic and safety is established.

evidence.speaker: social_worker
evidence.quote: He explicitly left the PHP against staff advice on day three of that program, which also contributed to his rapid return.

criteria.rationale: The patient recently failed a PHP (decompensated within nine days of discharge) and left that program against staff advice. The clinical team explicitly discussed why step-down to PHP or outpatient is premature given the medication titration, ongoing safety concerns, and absence of a safe home environment.
